@typescript-eslint/consistent-type-impor.mcp_query: typescript type only imports runtime dependency boundary
questions: Are runtime imports being used only for types?
Would type-only imports clarify the module's runtime dependency boundary?

@typescript-eslint/naming-convention.mcp_query: typescript naming consistency intention revealing names
questions: Is the name hiding domain meaning or using inconsistent terminology?
Would renaming improve call-site readability without changing behavior?

@typescript-eslint/no-confusing-void-exp.mcp_query: typescript confusing void expression separate side effects values
questions: Is this expression mixing side effects with value flow?
Would separating the side effect from the return value clarify intent?

@typescript-eslint/no-magic-numbers.mcp_query: typescript magic number named constant business policy
questions: Does this number encode business policy or a domain threshold?
Would a named constant make the rule searchable and easier to change?

@typescript-eslint/no-unnecessary-condit.mcp_query: typescript unnecessary condition dead branch explicit contract
questions: Is this branch dead or protecting against an impossible state?
Would removing it clarify the real contract?

@typescript-eslint/no-unnecessary-type-a.mcp_query: typescript unnecessary type assertion trusted shape contract
questions: Is the assertion hiding a type contract that is already known?
Would removing it make the trusted shape clearer?

@typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars.mcp_query: typescript unused variable dead code remove safely
questions: Is this dead code or a half-removed responsibility?
Can it be deleted safely without changing behavior?
Is falsy handling being confused with absence handling?
Would explicit nullish logic make the data contract clearer?

@typescript-eslint/strict-boolean-expres.mcp_query: typescript strict boolean expressions explicit predicate truthiness
questions: Is truthiness hiding the distinction between absence, empty values, and false?
Would an explicit predicate or comparison make the condition safer to read?

@typescript-eslint/switch-exhaustiveness.mcp_query: typescript exhaustive switch discriminated union explicit states
questions: Is variant handling incomplete or scattered?
Would an exhaustive branch make the domain states explicit?

clean-code/no-boolean-flag-arguments.mcp_query: typescript boolean flag argument intention revealing functions
questions: Does the boolean select behavior rather than represent plain data?
Would intention-revealing functions make call sites clearer?

clean-code/no-business-policy-literals.mcp_query: typescript business policy literal named constant domain state
questions: Does this literal encode business policy or domain state?
Would a named constant or typed domain value make changes safer?

clean-code/no-commented-out-code.mcp_query: typescript commented out code remove obsolete implementation
questions: Is this commented code obsolete implementation detail?
Can it be removed in favor of version control history or a tracked task?

clean-code/no-noisy-comments.mcp_query: typescript noisy comments remove visual separators byline comments
questions: Is the comment adding constraint/context or just visual noise?
Would deleting or replacing it with a precise explanation improve scanability?

clean-code/no-output-argument-mutation.mcp_query: typescript output argument mutation return value contract
questions: Is mutation hidden behind an output parameter?
Would returning a value make the contract clearer?

clean-code/no-redundant-comment.mcp_query: typescript redundant comment intention revealing code
questions: Does the comment repeat the next line instead of explaining why?
Can clearer naming or deletion remove the need for the comment?

clean-code/no-train-wrecks.mcp_query: typescript train wreck law of demeter object navigation
questions: Is this chain exposing too much object structure to the caller?
Would a named query/helper or domain method reduce coupling?

clean-code/todo-format.mcp_query: typescript todo comment issue id actionable technical debt
questions: Is this TODO actionable and traceable?
Should the comment include a tracked issue or be converted into code/tests?

complexity.mcp_query: typescript high complexity function mixed responsibilities guard clauses
questions: Is this function mixing validation, transformation, branching, and side effects?
Can guard clauses or cohesive helper extraction reduce branching?

max-depth.mcp_query: typescript deeply nested conditionals guard clauses readability
questions: Is rightward drift hiding the normal path?
Can early returns, named predicates, or smaller functions flatten the flow?

max-lines.mcp_query: typescript large file single responsibility extraction boundaries
questions: Does this file contain multiple reasons to change?
Are there cohesive module boundaries that preserve the public API?

max-lines-per-function.mcp_query: typescript long function split validation transformation side effects
questions: Does this function do more than one job?
Can stable parsing, validation, transformation, or side-effect steps be separated?

max-params.mcp_query: typescript long parameter list object parameter call site clarity
questions: Do these parameters travel together as a named concept?
Would a typed object parameter improve call-site clarity?

no-empty.mcp_query: typescript empty block ignored behavior explicit handling
questions: Is this empty block hiding ignored behavior or an incomplete branch?
Should the code handle, document, or remove the branch explicitly?

no-negated-condition.mcp_query: typescript negated condition positive predicate guard clause
questions: Is the negative branch making the normal path harder to follow?
Would a positive predicate or guard clause clarify the condition?

no-nested-ternary.mcp_query: typescript nested ternary decision logic readability
questions: Is dense expression logic hiding a decision table or branching policy?
Would named intermediate values or ordinary branches improve readability?

no-restricted-syntax.mcp_query: typescript long import list module boundary readability
questions: Is this import list signaling an overly broad module boundary?
Would a narrower module, namespace import, or split dependency improve scanability?

no-useless-return.mcp_query: typescript useless return redundant control flow
questions: Is redundant control flow adding noise to the function ending?
Can the return path be simplified without changing behavior?

sonarjs/cognitive-complexity.mcp_query: typescript cognitive complexity named predicates decision logic
questions: Which branches force a reader to retain too much context?
Can conditions be named or decision logic isolated?

sonarjs/no-dead-store.mcp_query: typescript dead store incomplete refactor unused state
questions: Is this assignment dead code or evidence of an incomplete refactor?
Can the unused state or surrounding responsibility be removed?

sonarjs/no-duplicate-string.mcp_query: typescript duplicate string named constant policy drift
questions: Is this repeated string a domain value, message, or policy token?
Would naming it avoid drift between copies?
Is duplicated branching hiding a missing abstraction or a bug?
Can the condition or branch body be simplified safely?

sonarjs/no-identical-conditions.mcp_query: typescript identical conditions duplicated predicate branch bug
questions: Is repeated conditional logic hiding a copy/paste mistake or missing predicate?
Can the branching model be simplified safely?

sonarjs/no-identical-functions.mcp_query: typescript duplicated functions shared helper policy drift
questions: Is this real duplication or a coincidental framework shape?
Can a shared helper remove policy drift without hiding intent?

sonarjs/no-inverted-boolean-check.mcp_query: typescript inverted boolean check positive predicate naming
questions: Is inverted boolean logic making the condition harder to read?
Would a positive predicate or renamed boolean clarify intent?

unicorn/explicit-length-check.mcp_query: typescript explicit length check collection emptiness
questions: Is collection emptiness being expressed indirectly?
Would an explicit length comparison clarify the condition?

unicorn/no-negated-condition.mcp_query: typescript negated condition positive predicate guard clause
questions: Is the negative branch making the normal path harder to follow?
Would a positive predicate or guard clause clarify the condition?

unicorn/no-null.mcp_query: typescript null absence value explicit state contract
questions: Is null being used as an ambiguous absence value?
Does the surrounding code prefer undefined, option-like objects, or explicit states?

clean-code-boolean-flag-argument.mcp_query: python boolean flag argument intention revealing functions options object
questions: Does the boolean select behavior rather than represent plain data?
Would intention-revealing functions or an explicit options object make call sites clearer?

clean-code-business-policy-literal.mcp_query: python business policy literal named constant domain state
questions: Does this literal encode business policy or domain state?
Would a named constant or typed domain value make changes safer?

clean-code-commented-out-code.mcp_query: python commented out code remove obsolete implementation
questions: Is this commented code obsolete implementation detail?
Can it be removed in favor of version control history or a tracked task?

clean-code-noisy-comment.mcp_query: python noisy comments remove visual separators byline comments
questions: Is the comment adding constraint/context or just visual noise?
Would deleting or replacing it with a precise explanation improve scanability?

clean-code-output-argument-mutation.mcp_query: python output argument mutation return value contract
questions: Is mutation hidden behind an output parameter?
Would returning a value or copying data make the contract clearer?

clean-code-redundant-comment.mcp_query: python redundant comment intention revealing code
questions: Does the comment repeat the next line instead of explaining why?
Can clearer naming or deletion remove the need for the comment?

clean-code-todo-format.mcp_query: python todo comment issue id actionable technical debt
questions: Is this TODO actionable and traceable?
Should the comment include a tracked issue or be converted into code/tests?

clean-code-train-wreck.mcp_query: python train wreck law of demeter object navigation
questions: Is this chain exposing too much object structure to the caller?
Would a named query/helper or domain method reduce coupling?

cyclic-import.mcp_query: python cyclic import module boundary dependency inversion
questions: Is the cycle exposing a module boundary problem?
Can dependencies be inverted or shared types/constants moved lower?

duplicate-code.mcp_query: python duplicate code shared helper policy drift
questions: Is this real domain duplication or a benign framework shape?
Can a shared helper remove policy drift without obscuring the caller?

too-few-public-methods.mcp_query: python too few public methods unnecessary class dataclass function
questions: Is this class just a passive data container or unnecessary wrapper?
Would a dataclass, TypedDict, or plain function fit the local style better?

too-many-ancestors.mcp_query: python too many ancestors inheritance composition coupling
questions: Is inheritance making behavior harder to trace?
Would composition or a flatter design reduce coupling?

too-many-arguments.mcp_query: python too many arguments dataclass typed dict call site clarity
questions: Do these arguments form a stable concept?
Would a dataclass, TypedDict, or keyword-only boundary clarify the call site?

too-many-boolean-expressions.mcp_query: python too many boolean expressions named predicates policy clarity
questions: Is this condition encoding several decisions at once?
Would named predicates or explicit decision steps make the policy clearer?

too-many-branches.mcp_query: python too many branches guard clauses named predicates
questions: Is branching mixing policy decisions with execution?
Can guard clauses, named predicates, or extracted decisions simplify the flow?

too-many-instance-attributes.mcp_query: python too many instance attributes class responsibility state groups
questions: Is this object accumulating multiple responsibilities or state groups?
Can cohesive state move into smaller value objects or collaborators?

too-many-lines.mcp_query: python large module single responsibility extraction boundaries
questions: Does this module have more than one reason to change?
Are there cohesive extraction boundaries that preserve imports and public APIs?

too-many-locals.mcp_query: python too many locals split parsing calculation side effects
questions: Is the function carrying too much intermediate state?
Can parsing, calculation, and side effects be separated?

too-many-nested-blocks.mcp_query: python nested blocks guard clauses main path readability
questions: Is nesting hiding the main path through the function?
Can early returns or named predicates flatten the code?

too-many-public-methods.mcp_query: python class too many public methods single responsibility
questions: Does this class represent more than one responsibility?
Can cohesive behavior move into smaller collaborators or plain functions?

too-many-return-statements.mcp_query: python too many return statements guard clauses result contract
questions: Are return paths representing distinct outcomes that should be named?
Can guard clauses or result objects make the contract clearer?

too-many-statements.mcp_query: python too many statements split validation transformation side effects
questions: Does this function combine validation, transformation, and side effects?
Can smaller named steps preserve behavior while improving scanability?

ARG001.mcp_query: python unused function argument narrow function boundary
questions: Is this unused argument required by a framework or callback contract?
If not, can the function boundary be narrowed?

ARG002.mcp_query: python unused method argument simplify contract
questions: Is this unused method argument required by inheritance or framework convention?
If not, can the method contract be simplified?

ERA001.mcp_query: python commented out code remove obsolete implementation
questions: Is this commented code obsolete implementation detail?
Can it be removed in favor of version control history or a tracked task?

Can the unused state or surrounding responsibility be removed?

PLR0915.mcp_query: python too many statements split validation transformation side effects

PLR0916.mcp_query: python too many boolean expressions named predicates policy clarity

PLR1702.mcp_query: python nested blocks guard clauses main path readability

PLR2004.mcp_query: python magic value comparison named constant business policy
questions: Does this value encode business policy or a domain threshold?
Would a named constant make the rule searchable and easier to change?

RET505.mcp_query: python unnecessary else after return flatten control flow
questions: Is the else branch unnecessary after a return?
Would flattening the control flow make the main path clearer?

RET506.mcp_query: python unnecessary else after raise flatten error handling
questions: Is the else branch unnecessary after an exception?
Would flattening the control flow make failure handling clearer?

RET507.mcp_query: python unnecessary else after continue flatten loop
questions: Is the else branch unnecessary after continue?
Would flattening the loop body improve scanability?

RET508.mcp_query: python unnecessary else after break flatten loop
questions: Is the else branch unnecessary after break?

SIM102.mcp_query: python nested if combined condition named predicate
questions: Is nested branching hiding a single combined condition?
Would a named predicate or combined guard clarify the decision?

SIM103.mcp_query: python needless boolean return named predicate
questions: Is a boolean branch returning literal booleans instead of the condition?
Would returning a named predicate make intent clearer?

SIM108.mcp_query: python if else assignment decision helper readability
questions: Is assignment spread across branches obscuring one decision?

TD002.mcp_query: python todo owner tracked technical debt
questions: Is this TODO owned by a person or team?
Should the debt be tracked or removed?

TD003.mcp_query: python todo issue link actionable technical debt
questions: Is this TODO tied to a tracked issue?
